violations.text: Food items stored in the top drawer of the backups cold drawer storage unit on the cookline were observed held at 43-44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All food items were all relocated to the walk-in cooler at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

At the time of inspection, scoops used for dispensing refrigerated foods along the cookline were observed stored with handles touching the food. Utensils being used to dispense food items that are stored in the food item are to be stored in the FOOD with their handles above the top of the FOOD and the container. Scoops were removed and stored properly at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris... - shelving in the walk-in cooler - dry ingredients containers - cookline hood screens Nonfood contact surfaces are to be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and preclude the build of soil and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2025

Kitchen floor tile grout lines were observed to have a buildup of soil residue and/or food debris. Facilities are to be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2025

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (bacon) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Employee voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the slicer have an accumulation of food residue. Employee washed and sanitized the slicer at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024
